Release.Name: Monty on the Run - D'n'B Edit [2sid]

Event.Name: Gubbdata 2020
Event.Tagline: Det var kanske inte bättre förr, men det är i alla fall sämre nu. / Maybe things weren't better back in the days, but it's never the less worse now.

Group.Name: MultiStyle Labs
Group.Trivia: Nothing but a bunch of friends annoying you in their spare time.
Group.Slogan: we ar moor inteligend then yu

Group.Name: Genesis Project
Group.Trivia:
[IMG]https://i.imgur.com/xQ1uaEd.png [/IMG] Genesis Project is a multi platform demo group and one of the most well known and productive C64 cracking groups of all time, formed in late 1985 or early 1986 on the C64 by <scener id=6392> (TNT), later <scener id=315>.
in 1993 a lot of the members strated cracking for SNES/PC etc. (See <release id=261018>. - SNES: L.A.Style, Tyger, Red Skin, Airwolf, Lexi, Zammbo. PC: Snacky)
A full detailed history 1986-2016 can be read in <release id=150629> (2016).
[url=https://demozoo.org/groups/3170/]The history of Genesis Project and further information.[/url]
Current (2024) G*P WHQ: <bbs id=103>, EHQ: <bbs id=1139>, and USHQ: <bbs id=320>. G*P also had <bbs id=114>, <bbs id=221>, <bbs id=42>, <bbs id=182>, <bbs id=113>, <bbs id=286>, <bbs id=39>, <bbs id=68> and <bbs id=218> as HQs.
G*P were the organizer of successful parties such as <event id=445>, <event id=128>, <event id=1846>, <event id=2316>, <event id=2879> and <event id=2756>.

Handle.HandleStory: I was a huge fan of the Dragonlance books.

Scener.Trivia:
Pre-scene, Raistlin was a prolific POKEster (ZZAP!, Commodore User, CCI, What Poke and many more).
Joined the demo scene in 1988 with his brother, Kreator (<scener id=7835>), creating SCC (Silsden Cracking Crew) .. soon after joined Pyrotech and, later, co-formed Excess.
Joined Genesis Project (G*P) - which became his proper "home" - after short stints in a few other groups.
Quit the scene in 1991, after Delirious 10, to focus on University studies.
27 years later, in 2018, he rejoined G*P and released Delirious 11 at Gubbdata 2018 - which took 2nd place. Later that year, with just a 3-month development window, released X Marks the Spot at X2018 (which took 4th place).
Raistlin has been in the games industry since 1994 and has worked on many games - Destruction Derby, the Test Drive series, LA Rush, Wheelman, Infinity Blade, the Gears of War series, Fortnite, Lawbreakers, Conan Exiles, Sea of Thieves and more... as well as working on Unreal Engine.
Moved to Thailand in 2021.
Created several C64 related websites:-
https://c64demo.com : a coding blog https://c64gfx.com : a site collecting C64 artworks https://sidquake.c64demo.com : a tool for easy packaging of SIDs as executables with visualiser, scroller etc https://dirquake.c64demo.com : a tool - mostly for C64GFX.com - to quickly grab screenshots of C64 DIR art

Handle.HandleStory: Originally created as an alter-ego when submitting POKEs to Zzap!64.

Handle.AKA: ZuRück :TheRyk, Ryker, Ryk, ZeRayk, SeRück, DerRyk, Rykky Martin, Rykster, rayger, raigä, Rykten, Ryksinspektor AD, TheGlytch, Ryk Astley

Scener.Trivia: Trust me. I know what I'm doing. Too many errors. Goodbye. Produced too much code. Old dog old tryx

UserComment.Text: Wow! Really great version of the tune. Sounds so good! :)

Scener.Trivia:
Was at the brim of the scene back in the days, knew a few fine gents...
Nowadays, I preserve old magnetic-media as somewhat of a hobby, trying to help the scene recollecting those hard to find bits..
Had a somewhat finished graphic production for datastorm 2017, but hardware malfunction made me loose all the work I did unfortunantly.. Hopefully I'll find the motivation and time to produce another one, for any future party.
Currently testing out stuff for GP, have worked as a software tester before, so might come in handy :)
Joined Genesis Project as tester in 2017.

UserComment.Text: this shows how 2 sid is more than 6 channels.

Scener.Trivia:
Got his first c64 in 1986. Did some c64-swapping 89-91, until he followed Jack Tramiel to the Atari ST (handles Mr.Marvel and Julgran until 1993). 2009 c64 saved him back to the right path. In 2010 he joined Genesis Project as graphician and helped Sixx rebuilding the group. Quickly he was doing more original supplies than gfx, and became the main original supplier of GP the same year. In 2011 he became GP co-organizer and 2012 main organizer.
He is also into preserving old releases, and between 2010 and 2025 he uploaded ~28 000 releases, and added ~2400 sceners, ~100 BBSs, and about 1200 groups to CSDb.
Organizer of the successful Gubbdata parties in Lund 2012-2022, Sweden. Also co-organized the Datastorm parties, and the last LCP.

UserComment.Text: So awesome!!! Thanx MCH. Sorry for fucking it up on the Gubbdata stream, I will discipline my 2sid machine. It needs some paddlin'.
Woooww , what a smasher, since i am an really bad fan of the hubbard sid this one makes me nearly cry ! Kewl Remix ! 10/10 !!!!

Handle.HandleStory: (This is the first nickname I've ever been using at PC scene, starting from mid-90s.)
(This is the nickname I've been using during the peak of my activity at PC scene as a chiptune musician.)
Replaced my old handle (CRD) using the same letters.
This is my real firstname. Although I have visited a few demoparties in Poland, I am NOT <scener id=4254>/Agony Design, nor was I in the scene during the early 1990s or before that.

UserComment.Text: Seriously good solid work here man! Well done indeed! I can imagine other Hubbard songs being DnB'd quite well too. :)

Handle.HandleStory: Derived from Amiga Lemmings game character.

Scener.Trivia:
When I was around 7, my uncle Jasiek (RIP) bought Commodore C16, equipped with datasette (of course!) I instantly fell in love with that toy, but because I couldn't have it, I've nicked all his Bajtek magazines (polish equivalent of Byte Magazine) instead  I could hardly read but somehow all the listings of programs seem to look nice to me. Few years later my dad brought my very own Commodore C64 breadbox from Germany (actually, I still need to ask where did he get it from, I suppose fleamarket)  I've had played games for a few months but I quickly got bored with them. I felt it deeply in my gut  I needed to code.
I begged my father again for weeks till he got me 1541-II disk drive; it costed something about 1,999,999 zł those days (yes, it was nearly 2 million polish zlotys!) I was in heaven  started swapping 5,25" disks with stangers on pirates' local markets, getting more stuff. It occasionally happened that a disk would contain a demo. Woah! Certainly that was far more interesting than some lousy shoot 'em ups. Also, I've bought absolutely every issue of C&A (Commodore & Amiga  hi Jetboy) and Kebab magazine (hi and thanks to Polonus and Silver Dream)  these were the best titles on the market  and I've learnt how to program in Basic 2.0.
Few years later (I was about 13 then) I started exploring MOS 6502 and the whole world of assembler. I found about demoscene, called myself Digger and founded my own group called Atlantic and went to some crazy booze copy/demo parties to win/lose some demo competitions. I was also shown how to use tracker and write SID 6581 music (thanks Ivanhoe) for the first time. Exciting times they were, we've hardly slept for 3 days! Few people had a huge impact on me those days (thanks Carrion, Shogoon, Brush and Hain), few helped me a lot (thank you Toaster, Branzol, Morris and Druid). Please remember no one had heard about internet, BBS was a luxury so we had to swap disks via post (snailmail). I always used to attach a few pages written by hand, can't imagine how I've done that then  it's hard to find time to write an email nowadays! Sincere thanks to Wozio for giving me a hard lesson how not to be an arse!

UserComment.Text: Amazing execution and great filtering Michał 🤘🏻 Had no idea DnB would sound so good on C64.

The remix that got "remixed" first timed played on stream. :D Glad we got to hear it properly played too.
Very faithful recreation of the original sounds with those fat MCH D&B grooves on top, just awesome!

Handle.HandleStory: a short and not so pretentious version from handle Carrion

Why psych858o? Just listen to some of my earliest tunes like Schizeralize and you'll find out ;)
Beside that, 8580 was my first and true love. I've been loyal until now, and I'm pretty sure that nothing will change that.

Scener.Trivia:
Handle change from Mc Moneysack to Steel mentioned in <release id=212933>.
Co-Founder of Success
Left the scene in 1993 and became a renowned graffiti artist.
Came back to the scene in 2016.

UserComment.Text: damn i got a boner. awesome tune mch !!!!!
Great work by MCH, really enjoying this!
